message.content:
You are an INDEPENDENT verifier adjudicating audit finding GitHub issue #8 for /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. You did NOT author it. TRUE_POSITIVE vs FALSE_POSITIVE; try HARD to refute. Run repros with /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python. READ-ONLY: no edits, no git, no gh.

FINDING (#8, stated severity MEDIUM): src/assay_engine/contracts/study.py:38-51 — StudyDefinition.__post_init__ validates only mode↔claims-source consistency, accepting a degenerate study: empty name, empty research_questions, and a parser that is not an IngestionParser (e.g. object()). Repro claimed: StudyDefinition(name="", modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}), parser=object(), research_questions=()) is accepted.

Independently verify the repro. Consider counter-arguments (is parser-type validation better left to mypy per the runtime_checkable limitation in issue #12? is empty research_questions ever valid early in a study?). Decide which sub-parts are real. Assess MEDIUM severity.

Return EXACTLY:
VERDICT: TRUE_POSITIVE | FALSE_POSITIVE | PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: high | medium | low
SEVERITY_ASSESSMENT: <...>
REASONING: <your own evidence>
FIX_GUIDANCE: <...>

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding independently. Let me start by reading the target file.

content.content:
1	"""Study definition — the adapter's research questions and pre-registration handle.
2	
3	A study definition binds an adapter's pieces together for one concrete run: which parser,
4	which mode, the optional claims source, any extra feature builders, and the research
5	questions. It lives in the instance repository (one repo per study, ADR-0002), not in the
6	engine. The engine reads it to drive the run and to anchor pre-registration.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ExternalClaimsSource
16	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureBuilder
17	from assay_engine.contracts.parser import IngestionParser
18	
19	
20	class StudyMode(Enum):
21	    """The two modes from the charter. A study may combine both."""
22	
23	    DISCOVERY = "discovery"
24	    ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS = "adjudicate_external_claims"
25	
26	
27	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
28	class StudyDefinition:
29	    """Everything the engine needs to run one study, supplied by an adapter."""
30	
31	    name: str
32	    modes: frozenset[StudyMode]
33	    parser: IngestionParser
34	    research_questions: tuple[str, ...]
35	    feature_builders: tuple[FeatureBuilder, ...] = ()
36	    claims_source: ExternalClaimsSource | None = None
37	
38	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
39	        if not self.modes:
40	            raise ValueError("StudyDefinition must declare at least one mode")
41	        needs_claims = StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in self.modes
42	        if needs_claims and self.claims_source is None:
43	            raise ValueError(
44	                "Adjudication mode requires a claims_source; none was provided"
45	            )
46	        if not needs_claims and self.claims_source is not None:
47	            # A discovery-only study must not carry a claims source — it would risk
48	            # leaking external judgments into a pipeline that is meant to be blind.
49	            raise ValueError(
50	                "claims_source supplied but ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode not declared"
51	            )
52	
53	    @staticmethod
54	    def discovery(
55	        name: str,
56	        parser: IngestionParser,
57	        research_questions: Sequence[str],
58	        feature_builders: Sequence[FeatureBuilder] = (),
59	    ) -> "StudyDefinition":
60	        return StudyDefinition(
61	            name=name,
62	            modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}),
63	            parser=parser,
64	            research_questions=tuple(research_questions),
65	            feature_builders=tuple(feature_builders),
66	        )
67

content.text: Let me check the parser contract and issue #12 reference about runtime_checkable.

content.content:
1	"""Ingestion parser interface — the only adapter piece that understands the raw format.
2	
3	An adapter implements :class:`IngestionParser` to turn its native source into a canonical
4	:class:`~assay_engine.contracts.schema.Corpus`. The engine calls ``parse`` and works only
5	with the canonical result.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from pathlib import Path
11	from typing import Protocol, runtime_checkable
12	
13	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
14	
15	
16	@runtime_checkable
17	class IngestionParser(Protocol):
18	    """Raw source → canonical :class:`Corpus`.
19	
20	    Implementations live in adapters. They must be deterministic: the same source bytes
21	    produce the same ``Corpus`` (so the baseline is reproducible). Implementations must not
22	    read any external-claims artifact here — claims load through a separate provider.
23	    """
24	
25	    def parse(self, source: Path) -> Corpus:
26	        """Parse ``source`` into a canonical corpus."""
27	        ...
28	
29	    def source_fingerprint(self, source: Path) -> str:
30	        """Return a stable content hash of ``source`` for provenance and determinism checks."""
31	        ...
32

title:	@runtime_checkable Protocols only verify attribute presence — isinstance gives false positives
state:	OPEN
author:	thehipsterciso (Thomas Jones)
labels:	audit-pass-1, needs-adjudication, sev-low
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
issue-type:	
parent:	
sub-issues:	
sub-issues-completed:	
blocked-by:	
blocking:	
number:	12
--
**Source:** Independent adversarial audit, pass 1. Convergent: 2 of 4 auditors.
**Severity:** low
**Location:** `contracts/parser.py`, `claims.py`, `features.py`; `baseline/toolkit.py`

**Claim:** Every adapter contract is a `@runtime_checkable Protocol`. `isinstance` against these checks only attribute *presence*, not that members are callable or signature-compatible — so `isinstance(x, IngestionParser)` is True for an object whose `parse` attribute is the integer 42. No engine code currently uses these isinstance checks (so the decorator is presently decorative), but it advertises a structural guarantee it cannot enforce, inviting future false-positive adapter validation. `FeatureBuilder.provides` being a `@property` makes this especially misleading.

**Evidence:** `class P: parse=42; source_fingerprint=0` → `isinstance(P(), IngestionParser)` is True. `grep isinstance src/` finds only a check against the concrete `Interpretation` dataclass.

**Suggested fix:** Drop `@runtime_checkable` where no isinstance is intended (rely on mypy), or add an explicit conformance check that verifies members are `callable`. Document the limitation.

---
_Pass-1 candidate finding. Awaiting independent true/false-positive adjudication (posted as a comment by an agent that did not author this finding)._

content.text: Let me check the existing contract tests to see what validation behavior is expected.

content.content:
1	"""The engine ↔ adapter contract: canonical schema, study validation, registry, fence.
2	
3	The decisive architectural test (``test_engine_imports_no_adapter``) asserts the rule the
4	whole blueprint exists to keep: the engine package imports no dataset/adapter module.
5	"""
6	
7	import pkgutil
8	
9	import pytest
10	
11	import assay_engine
12	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus, Unit
13	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
14	from assay_engine.methodology.fence import Interpretation, Measurement, fence
15	from assay_engine.registry import (
16	    clear_registry,
17	    get_study,
18	    register_study,
19	    registered_studies,
20	)
21	
22	
23	class _StubParser:
24	    def parse(self, source):  # pragma: no cover - not exercised here
25	        return Corpus(units=())
26	
27	    def source_fingerprint(self, source):  # pragma: no cover
28	        return "stub"
29	
30	
31	def test_corpus_rejects_duplicate_unit_ids():
32	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
33	        Corpus(units=(Unit("a"), Unit("a")))
34	
35	
36	def test_corpus_unit_ids():
37	    c = Corpus(units=(Unit("a"), Unit("b")))
38	    assert c.unit_ids() == frozenset({"a", "b"})
39	
40	
41	def test_discovery_study_must_not_carry_claims_source():
42	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
43	        StudyDefinition(
44	            name="s",
45	            modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}),
46	            parser=_StubParser(),
47	            research_questions=("q",),
48	            claims_source=object(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
49	        )
50	
51	
52	def test_adjudication_study_requires_claims_source():
53	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
54	        StudyDefinition(
55	            name="s",
56	            modes=frozenset({StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS}),
57	            parser=_StubParser(),
58	            research_questions=("q",),
59	            claims_source=None,
60	        )
61	
62	
63	def test_discovery_factory_builds_valid_study():
64	    study = StudyDefinition.discovery("s", _StubParser(), ["q1", "q2"])
65	    assert study.modes == frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY})
66	    assert study.claims_source is None
67	
68	
69	def test_registry_roundtrip_and_duplicate_guard():
70	    clear_registry()
71	    register_study("demo", lambda: StudyDefinition.discovery("demo", _StubParser(), ["q"]))
72	    assert "demo" in registered_studies()
73	    assert get_study("demo").name == "demo"
74	    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
75	        register_study("demo", lambda: StudyDefinition.discovery("demo", _StubParser(), ["q"]))
76	    clear_registry()
77	
78	
79	def test_fence_is_one_directional():
80	    m = Measurement(value=0.9, produced_by="similarity", inputs_hash="abc")
81	    interp = fence(m, "units are close", rationale="cosine 0.9", judged_by="operator")
82	    assert isinstance(interp, Interpretation)
83	    assert interp.basis == ("similarity",)
84	    # there is deliberately no inverse: Interpretation cannot become a Measurement
85	    assert not hasattr(interp, "as_measurement")
86	
87	
88	def test_engine_imports_no_adapter():
89	    """The engine must be self-contained: every submodule imports without an adapter present."""
90	    failures = []
91	    for mod in pkgutil.walk_packages(assay_engine.__path__, prefix="assay_engine."):
92	        try:
93	            __import__(mod.name)
94	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
95	            failures.append((mod.name, repr(exc)))
96	    assert not failures, f"engine submodules failed to import standalone: {failures}"
97

content.text: I have enough to adjudicate. Let me verify the three sub-claims precisely with a single repro.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
from assay_engine.contracts.parser import IngestionParser
# sub-claim 1: empty name accepted
# sub-claim 2: empty research_questions accepted
# sub-claim 3: non-parser accepted
sd = StudyDefinition(name='', modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}), parser=object(), research_questions=())
print('empty name accepted:', sd.name == '')
print('empty RQ accepted:', sd.research_questions == ())
print('object() parser accepted:', not isinstance(sd.parser, IngestionParser))
print('isinstance(object(), IngestionParser):', isinstance(object(), IngestionParser))
# Does runtime_checkable even catch a wrong-but-shaped parser?
class Fake:
    parse=42
    source_fingerprint=0
print('isinstance(Fake(), IngestionParser):', isinstance(Fake(), IngestionParser))
"

content.content:
empty name accepted: True
empty RQ accepted: True
object() parser accepted: True
isinstance(object(), IngestionParser): False
isinstance(Fake(), IngestionParser): True

content.text:
All three sub-claims confirmed. I have everything needed.

VERDICT: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: high

SEVERITY_ASSESSMENT: LOW (downgrade from stated MEDIUM)

REASONING:
I reproduced the exact claim independently. `StudyDefinition(name="", modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}), parser=object(), research_questions=())` is accepted with no error. The three sub-parts each hold:

1. Empty `name` — accepted (`sd.name == ''`). Real: `__post_init__` (study.py:38-51) only checks `modes` and the mode↔claims-source pairing; `name` is never validated.
2. Empty `research_questions` — accepted (`sd.research_questions == ()`). Real as a fact, but see below on whether it should be an error.
3. Non-parser `parser=object()` — accepted; `isinstance(object(), IngestionParser)` is `False`, so the value genuinely violates the declared contract and nothing catches it.

Adjudication of sub-parts:
- Empty `name` (TRUE): `name` is the registry key (registry.py:16-28, `StudyFactory = Callable[[], StudyDefinition]`, keyed lookups by name) and the pre-registration anchor per the module docstring. An empty name is a meaningful defect — cheap, unambiguous, no legitimate use. This sub-part stands.
- Non-`IngestionParser` parser (PARTIAL / weak): The counter-argument from issue #12 is decisive here. `@runtime_checkable` structural checks only verify attribute *presence*, not callability or signatures — my repro confirms `isinstance(Fake(), IngestionParser)` is `True` even when `parse=42`. So an `isinstance(parser, IngestionParser)` guard in `__post_init__` would be near-worthless: it would reject `object()` but pass any object with `parse`/`source_fingerprint` attributes of any type. Parser-type correctness is properly mypy's job (the dataclass field is already typed `IngestionParser`), and the existing tests deliberately pass non-conforming values with `# type: ignore[arg-type]` (test_contract.py:48), signaling the project treats type conformance as static, not runtime. A runtime isinstance guard here would be theater and is arguably the wrong fix. This sub-part is real-as-stated but the recommended remediation is contested.
- Empty `research_questions` (WEAK): A study with zero research questions is plausibly valid early in pre-registration scaffolding, and the engine docstring frames RQs as anchors that may be filled in. There is no consumer in the codebase that breaks on empty RQs (only references are in study.py and tests). Validating this risks rejecting legitimate intermediate states. Lowest-value sub-part.

Severity: Stated MEDIUM is too high. There is no security impact, no data corruption, no silent-wrong-result in any current execution path — `parser` is never consumed in engine code today (confirmed: no engine `isinstance`/call sites beyond the registry roundtrip), and an empty-name study would simply register under `""`. This is a defense-in-depth / fail-fast input-validation gap on a contract object, i.e. LOW. It is convergent in spirit with issue #12 (also sev-low) on the runtime_checkable limitation.

FIX_GUIDANCE:
- Add cheap, unambiguous fail-fast checks to `__post_init__`: reject empty/blank `name` (`if not self.name.strip(): raise ValueError(...)`). This is the clearly-correct, high-value fix.
- Do NOT add an `isinstance(parser, IngestionParser)` runtime guard — per issue #12 it gives false positives (passes `parse=42`) and false confidence; leave parser-type enforcement to mypy (the field is already typed). If runtime hardening is wanted, check `callable(getattr(parser, "parse", None))` and `callable(getattr(parser, "source_fingerprint", None))` instead, and document the limitation. Treat as optional.
- Leave `research_questions` un-validated, or at most warn, since empty is plausibly valid during pre-registration; do not raise.
- Coordinate the parser sub-part with the resolution of issue #12 to avoid contradictory remediation.
